thesis: AbbVie is a global biopharmaceutical compounder completing the handoff from Humira to Skyrizi and Rinvoq. **[Fact]** FY2025 net revenues were **$61.2 billion**; owner cash (operating cash flow less capital spending) was about **$10.05 per diluted share**. **[Fact]** Skyrizi rose **50%** and Rinvoq **39%** in FY2025 while Humira fell **49%**. **[Inference]** At ~**$250** per share the Lawrence base case implies about **6.9% per year** on normalized owner cash, below our accumulate hurdle; proof-first component math sums to **~$250 per share** of economic value.
why_market_wrong: There is **no dated mispricing catalyst** (HK predictive attribute: none). **[Inference]** Generalists may anchor on Humira collapse and depressed GAAP earnings (**$2.36** diluted EPS) without normalizing intangible amortization and litigation items. **[Fact]** Q1 2026 revenue grew **12.4%** year over year with immunology still scaling. **[Inference]** The market may be paying for visible dividend growth (**$6.65** annual) and immunology momentum, leaving little margin of safety at spot.

key_assumptions.source: FY2025 OCF $19.03B less capex $1.21B / 1,773M shares (10-K filed 2026-02-20)
Lawrence base scenario; Skyrizi/Rinvoq offset Humira erosion
Five additive proofs in valuation.json (contract backfill 2026-07-21)

open_questions: Indication-level pipeline probability trees remain aggregate judgments.
Humira antitrust and IRA pricing trajectory could widen downside reserve.
No approved third-party sources indexed for numeric blend.
